story_prompt: create a lecture on javascript function

scenes.text: In this introduction, we explore what JavaScript functions are and why they are fundamental concepts in programming. Functions are reusable blocks of code designed to perform a particular task. They help make programs modular and easier to manage.
scenes.image_prompt: mindmap; root((JavaScript Functions)); Concept 1; Concept 2; Concept 3;

scenes.text: Here we describe how to define a function. A function is usually defined using the 'function' keyword followed by a name, parameters, a set of instructions, and a return statement.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Begin] --> B[Define Function]; B --> C[Write Instructions]; C --> D[Return Value]; D --> E[End];

scenes.text: In this part, we examine how function invocation works. A function must be called or invoked to execute its code. This can be done by referencing the function's name with the required parameters if any.
scenes.image_prompt: sequenceDiagram; participant User; participant Function; User->>Function: Call; Function-->>User: Response;

scenes.text: This section focuses on function parameters and returns. Functions can accept input values called parameters and can produce an output known as the return value.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Parameters Input]; B --> C[Function Execution]; C --> D[Return Output]; D --> E[End];

scenes.text: Here we discuss advanced concepts like function expressions and arrow functions, which offer alternative ways to write functions. Understanding these allows developers to write more concise and expressive code.
scenes.image_prompt: gantt; title Function Conversions; dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD; section Function Forms; Function Declaration: A1, 1d; Function Expression: A2, after A1, 1d; Arrow Function: A3, after A2, 1d;
